data.title: Capri looks ahead after closing on the Versace sale
data.description:
Prada Group (OTCPK:PRDSY) finalized its $1.25 billion acquisition of Versace from Capri Holdings (CPRI) this week.

Capri Holdings (CPRI), which bought Versace for $2 billion in 2018, offloaded the brand amid post-pandemic struggles, as Versace's revenues fell 15% to $193 million in fiscal 2025 and clashed with "quiet luxury" trends. Meanwhile, Prada, fresh off a 17% revenue rise to €5.4 billion last year driven by Miu Miu, sees Versace adding 13% to its pro forma revenues while tapping untapped growth potential through Italian supply chain integration.​

Lorenzo Bertelli, Prada's marketing director and heir, will become Versace's executive chairman, with new creative director Dario Vitale, formerly of Miu Miu, leading the design team after Donatella Versace's earlier departure. 

Capri (CPRI) CEO John Idol said the company plans to use the proceeds from the Prada sale to repay the majority of its debt, which will substantially strengthen the balance sheet. He noted that closing on the transaction will significantly reduce Capri's (CPRI) leverage ratio and provide greater financial flexibility to both invest in growth and return capital to shareholders in the future. 

"We remain focused on executing our strategic initiatives across Michael Kors and Jimmy Choo to maximize the potential of our iconic brands. Looking ahead, we believe we are on track to stabilize our business this year while establishing a solid foundation for a return to growth in fiscal 2027," highlighted Idol.

Shares of Capri (CPRI) are up 19.5% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: National Bank of Canada raises dividend by 5.1% to CAD 1.24 a share
data.description:
• National Bank of Canada (NTIOF) declares CAD 1.24/share quarterly dividend, 5.1% increase from prior dividend of CAD 1.18.
  • Forward yield 2.89%
  • Payable Feb. 1; to shareholders of record Dec. 29; ex-div Dec. 29.
  • See NTIOF Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Dollar Tree Non-GAAP EPS of $1.21 beats by $0.13, revenue of $4.75B beats by $60M
data.description:
• Dollar Tree press release (DLTR): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.21 beats by $0.13.
  • Revenue of $4.75B (+9.4% Y/Y) beats by $60M.
  • Introducing Fourth Quarter Fiscal 2025 Outlook: 4% to 6% Comparable Store Net Sales Growth and $2.40 to $2.60 Adjusted EPS from Continuing Operations
  • Revising Full-Year Fiscal 2025 Comparable Store Net Sales Growth Outlook Range to 5.0% to 5.5% and Net Sales Outlook Range to $19.35 to $19.45 Billion
  • Increasing Full-Year Fiscal 2025 Adjusted EPS from Continuing Operations Outlook Range to $5.60 to $5.80 to Reflect Updated Operating Outlook and Year-to-Date Share Repurchases
  •

data.title: Thor GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.48, revenue of $2.39B beats by $340M
data.description:
• Thor press release (THO): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.48.
  • Revenue of $2.39B (+11.7% Y/Y) beats by $340M.

  • For fiscal 2026, the Company's full-year financial guidance includes:

    • Consolidated net sales in the range of $9.0 billion to $9.5 billion
    • Stable gross margin at midpoint, with upside in a stronger market
    • Diluted earnings per share in the range of $3.75 to $4.25
    • Guidance assumes a low- to mid-single digit retail decline in North America with stable market share

data.title: Ferrari renews partnership with Philip Morris
data.description:
• Ferrari N.V. (RACE) said that Ferrari S.p.A., its wholly-owned Italian subsidiary, has renewed a its multi-year partnership with Philip Morris (PM).
  • The agreement will take effect on January 1, 2026, making PM a premium partner of Scuderia Ferrari HP and a series partner of the Ferrari Challenge Trofeo Pirelli.

data.title: Ryde stock slides on offerings to raise $14M
data.description:
• Ryde Group (RYDE) shares fell nearly 7% premarket on Wednesday after the mobility app announced plans to raise around US$14 million.
  • The company entered into a securities purchase agreement with several investors for a private placement of 33.33M Class A ordinary shares and a registered direct offering of 5.55M Class A ordinary shares at an offering price of US$0.36 per class A share.
  • Ryde Group (RYDE) currently intends to use the net proceeds from the offerings for working capital and general corporate purposes. The registered direct offering and private placement are expected to close on or about December 3 and December 9, 2025, respectively.

data.title: IREN prices stock, debt offerings
data.description:
IREN (IREN) priced an offering of about 39.7 million shares at $41.12 each, raising roughly $1.63 billion.

Concurrently with the pricing, IREN entered into one or more separate, privately negotiated transactions with a limited number of holders of its outstanding 3.25% convertible senior notes due 2030 and 3.50% convertible senior notes due 2029 to repurchase around $227.7 million of the existing 2030 convertible notes and ~$316.6 million of the existing 2029 convertible notes for an aggregate repurchase price of around $1,632.4 million.

IREN also priced a private offering of $1 billion in 0.25% convertible notes due 2032 and $1 billion in 1.00% convertible notes due 2033, with an option for buyers to purchase up to an additional $150 million of each.

Proceeds from the offerings will help fund capped call transactions, buy back existing convertible debt, and support general corporate purposes. The transactions are expected to close on December 8, 2025.

IREN +1.79% premarket to $41.86

Source: Press Release

data.title: EU reaches deal to end Russian gas imports by late 2027
data.description:
The European Union has reached a deal to permanently stop Russian gas imports by November 2027 and phase out Russian oil, in an effort to end the bloc's energy dependence on Russia amid the Ukraine war. 

The deal ensures a gradual but permanent end of Russian gas imports, with LNG imports phased out by Dec. 31, 2026 and pipeline gas by Sept. 30, 2027.

EU member states may extend this deadline until Oct. 31, 2027 in case their storage levels are below the required filling levels. 

The EU remains committed to phase out all remaining oil imports from Russia by the end of 2027, and a legislative proposal to ban these imports will be tabled early next year.

EU member states must submit national diversification plans for oil and gas supplies by March 1, 2026. They must also notify the European Commission whether they have Russian gas supply contracts or national bans in place.

"By depleting Putin's war chest, we stand in solidarity with Ukraine and set our sights on new energy partnerships and opportunities for the sector," said Ursula von der Leyen, the commission's president.

Russian gas accounted for 13% of the EU's overall imports in the first half of 2025, down from 45% before Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

"All imports of Russian coal have been banned by sanctions, oil imports have shrunk from 27% at the beginning of 2022 to 2% now," the EU noted.
